entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Madrid 2025 - Mar 21, 2025
entry.content: Yes, we have sent confirmation emails already. There is a waitlist for this event as well. If you have RSVPed you should have received one or the other.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Madrid 2025 - Mar 21, 2025
entry.content: Looking forward to meeting you then.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Madrid 2025 - Mar 21, 2025
entry.content: We will also livestream the event 🤞.... you can check out the different times here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL2zNL146thBrORy076Jg33K2fyfq25is3

entry.title: 🇨🇭 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, AEM (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this 5-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Thursday, May 22nd 2025, in Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland at GAMBIT Consulting AG office (8th floor) .

The instructors for this event are Developer Advocate @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski . 

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page.

Location: Gambit Consulting AG, Suurstoffi 22, 6343 Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland (8th floor)

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Paris, France)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this 5-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Paris, France.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Tuesday, June 11th 2025, in Nagarro, Tour Franklin, 100-101 Terr. Boieldieu, 92800 Puteaux.

The instructors for this event are Developer Advocate @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski . 

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page.

Location: Nagarro, Tour Franklin, 100-101 Terr. Boieldieu, 92800 Puteaux

entry.title: Re: Configure webhook notifications from SAP Cloud ALM to SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content: Nice to know that this is possible. However, if possible, it might be best to send it to some form of message broker, e.g. Event Mesh capability or SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, so that the event can be easily distributed to many more consumers in case it is needed in the future.,

entry.title: Re: 🇪🇸 Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content: See you in a couple of days!

entry.title: TIL - How to force a specific version of SAP UI5 when running Fiori preview in a CAP project
entry.content:
Today, I was running a very simple CAP service, which is part of the Service integration with SAP Cloud Application Programming model CodeJam. When running the Fiori preview for an entity, I was always getting an error message in the console: TypeError: o.intersection is not a function. This error was raised by the Fiori preview independent of the entity that I was trying to preview. That made me thing that the issue was not with the entity but with the Fiori preview itself.


Fiori preview error

Based on the network requests that I was able to see in the Developer Tools, I knew it was using version 1.132.0 of SAP UI5. Then I thought… let’s force the project to use a different version to see if the error would go away. I debugged the CAP project and I knew exactly where was the version of SAP UI5 being set. I set the variable to 1.130.0 and the preview worked fine. Nice!


Debug forcing version

Now, I don’t want to debug the project every time and manually set the version…. digging deeper, I found out that the @sap/cds-fiori module has a package.json, where the version of SAP UI5 is set. It is well known that you can set project-specific configuration via a .env file. So, I created a .env file in the root of the project and
set the following content:

cds.fiori.preview.ui5.version=1.130.0

By setting that environment variable I’m forcing the project to use version 1.130.0 of SAP UI5 when running the Fiori preview. I restarted the project and the preview worked fine.


Fiori preview running specific version

Content originally published in https://ajmaradiaga.com/TIL-Force-UI5-version-in-Fiori-preview/

entry.title: And that's a wrap - SIT Madrid 2025
entry.content:
Last Friday, 21st of March, we restarted the SAP Inside Track "chapter" in Madrid. The day was full of interesting sessions, good food, and interesting networking opportunities.
 

 

Below, are the different talks that were part of the event:
  • 09:30 - Level up your CAP skills by learning how to use the cds repl ( @qmacro  )
  • 10:00 - Knowledge Graphs: From Fundamentals to Implementation with SAP S/4HANA and Enterprise Applications ( @Antonio_leites )
  • 10:30 - How SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions ( @maria_teresa_gutierrez )
  • 11:30 - SAP Business AI: Latest Breakthroughs and Future Prospects ( @salva_gimeno )
  • 12:00 - Code10 CI Accelerator - The Integration Suite Addon ( @Antonio_C10IT )
  • 12:30 - Building flexible Event-Driven Architectures with open standards ( @ajmaradiaga  )
  • 13:30 - Building a Personal AI Fitness Coach with SAP BTP, STRAVA, and Ollama GenAI ( @IgorNarodowy  )
  • 14:00 - Mastering Connectivity & Accelerating Integrations with prepackaged integration content ( @rcvblanco @and@sanderkool  )
  • 14:30 - SAP Customer Insights Dashboard ( @Lluis )

 

You weren't able to make it to the in-person event? That's not a problem. The entire day was live streamed on YouTube, meaning that you can watch the recordings of the different sessions.

Sessions from 09:30 - 11:00

Session from 11:30 - 13:00

Sessions from 13:30 - 15:00

Thanks to all our speakers and everyone who joined us in person and online. Also, thanks to our event sponsors: SAP, SAP Press, Sulzer, and Code10.

It was a great day and quite interesting to know other members of the SAP Community in Madrid. I'm already looking forward to the next SIT Madrid ;-).

entry.title: How to peek into queues in SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
entry.content:
In this short blog post, we will explore how to leverage JMSToolbox to connect to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM), and peek at events that are in a queue.

JMSToolbox

First of all, what is JMSToolbox? JMSToolbox is a JMS Client that allows us to easily connect to different Queue Managers/Queue available, e.g. SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh.

We can download JMSToolbox straight from the project's GitHub repository: https://github.com/jmstoolbox/jmstoolbox. We will need to download the binaries from the repository. Installation instructions may vary depending on the platform on which you will be running it.

AEM connection details

Before we can connect to AEM via JMSToolbox, we will need some details from our event broker service.

  • Broker connection details for the Solace Messaging protocol. Go to the Event Broker service you want to connect to > Connect tab > View by protocol and expand the Solace Messaging accordion. Select any language from the list, and the credentials should appear on the right side. From here, we need the host URI, username, and password.

  • Event broker service management details. We need the service management URL, VPN, username, and password. In the same event broker service go to the Manage tab > expand the Broker Manager - Web application accordion. The required details should appear on the screen. See the screenshot below.

     

Connecting to AEM in JMSToolbox

Now that we have the AEM connection details, we can proceed to configure JMSToolbox.

In JMSToolbox we create a new session and configure a connection to AEM. Here you will input the Session Name, Queue Manager, Host/Port (from the Solace Messaging connection details), and Security User id and password. The connection details will look similar to the ones in the screenshot below.

Before creating the session we will define some properties. Enter the event broker service management details in the respective fields - VPN, mgmt_url, mgmgt_username, mgmt_password, and then click the Create button. The session properties will look similar to the ones in the screenshot below.

Once configured, it will connect to AEM and automatically show all the queues available in the event broker service. Click on any queue to peek at the events available.

Messages in Queue

I hope this quick blog post help you troubleshoot/explore events in your AEM queues. You can learn more about JMSToolbox and AEM brokers (Solace) here.

entry.title: Re: Conditionally send a standard event from SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content: Great question @SumitKundu . Unfortunately, it is not possible at the moment to do "conditional" event publishing in case of a specific field being updated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠

First? 😁

Check out how the blur on the screenshot makes it look like the Matrix hehe.

entry.title: Re: 🇫🇷 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Paris, France)
entry.content: @marco-silvathis CodeJam is on the 11th of June. There is a separate CodeJam (SAP Build: Event-based processes) which will take place the day before.

entry.title: Re: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
You will be missed. Enjoy the next chapter in your life.

PS: We will be running a couple of CodeJams in Paris with @Dan_Wroblewski (early June). It will be great to meet you then 😉

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, May 30th, 2025 at the SAP office in Madrid 🇪🇸 from 09:30 - 14:30 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @noravonthenen.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event fro...
entry.content: In Cloud Integration you should be consuming from the queue directly, not subscribing to the topic. The queue is already subscribed to the topic and it will capture those events for you. This is why you received the events in Cloud Integration (because of the additional topic subscription) but the queue still has events. You are not consuming the events in the queue. In the sender adapter, configure the queue instead of the topic and it should work.

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core CodeJam
entry.content:
On the 30th of May, there will be an SAP CodeJam event on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

RSVP Here -> https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ec-p/14082043#M820

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, May 30th, 2025 at the SAP office in Madrid 🇪🇸 from 09:30 - 14:30 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English.

entry.title: Re: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event...
entry.content: @erza, if you have access to AEM, you might be able to complete the first part of the CodeJam. You will still be missing the S4HC mock system that sends events to AEM, but there is a public Docker image that you can run locally to send events to your AEM: https://hub.docker.com/r/ajmaradiaga/s4-mock-server-with-bp-created-events. It is the exact same service we are running for the CodeJam.

entry.title: Quedada SAP - post SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de la comunidad SAP en Madrid!

¿Que les parece si quedamos nuevamente? Pero esta vez para hablar un poco de lo que fue el SAP Sapphire 2025.

La Quedada SAP en Madrid se llevará a cabo el 29 de mayo de 2025 a las 18:00 hora local en la Cervecería Köln, que esta muy cerca de la nueva oficina de SAP.

La idea es juntarnos y compartir un poco sobre todo lo que es el mundo SAP. En esta ocasion compartir experiencias/opiniones del primer gran evento SAP del año - SAP Sapphire. Aparte de esto, tambien coincide que estas fechas esta @MustafaBensan (SAP Champion) por Madrid y se nos unira.

Por favor, haznos saber si participarás en la quedada haciendo clic en la seccion de RSVP. No tenemos una reserva como tal en el espacio pero es un lugar bastante amplio y podriamos juntar alli un par de mesas. Nota: Cada uno es responsable de comprar su propia comida o bebida si lo desea.

¡Nos vemos pronto!

-----

¡Hola SAP Community members in Madrid!

How about we meet again? But this time to chat about SAP TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

The SAP Meetup in Madrid will take place on May 29, 2025, at 18:00 local time at Cerveceria Köln, which is very close to the new SAP office.

The idea is to get together and share a bit about everything in the SAP world. This time around, share our experiences/opinions about the first big SAP event of the year - SAP Sapphire. Also, SAP Champion @MustafaBensan is in town and he will be joining us :-).

Please let us know if you will be participating in the meetup by clicking on the RSVP section. We do not have a reservation in the space, but it is fairly large, and we could put a couple of tables together there. Note: Everyone is responsible for purchasing their own food or drinks if they wish to.

See you soon!

Antonio Maradiaga
Developer Advocate @ SAP

entry.title: Re: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
Hi @Amarcos ,

Given that we have more RSVPs than places available, we will send confirmation emails 15 days before the event and manage a waitlist.

In your case, you were #28 to register for the event, meaning you will be on the waitlist.
